policy.purpose: refresh #507 blocker vocabulary with product-relevant probes before accepting #509 kernel changes

probes.reason: high-value but requires project dependency context before exact admission

probes.kernel_need: package occurrence plus default demand profile
probes.reason: Guava identity and default/callback boundaries are not both proven

probes.kernel_need: dependency proof plus receiver class/domain producer
probes.reason: raw present?/blank? names and metadata are not exact proof

probes.kernel_need: Go version proof and map iteration order boundary
probes.reason: language version metadata alone does not prove order-insensitive use
collect-like result type is caller-selected and must not be emitted as a fixed domain

probes.kernel_need: pull-lazy source and terminal consumption proof
probes.reason: chain construction alone does not prove exact materialized behavior

probes.kernel_need: observable lifecycle, scheduler, and callback effect substrate
probes.reason: generic demand/effect profiles do not prove observable semantics

probes.kernel_need: package proof and callback/shorthand iteratee model
probes.reason: raw selector and corpus presence are pricing signals only
DomainRequirement composition exists, but dtype evidence producer is missing

probes.kernel_need: Series domain proof and NA value semantics
probes.reason: table/series domain and missing-value behavior are not kernel facts

probes.kernel_need: admitted API result proof feeding terminal receiver proof
probes.reason: demand profiles exist; exact rollout still depends on admitted stream contracts

probes.kernel_need: admitted iterator result proof feeding terminal receiver proof
probes.reason: current HOF profiles cover timing but not all materialization or trait identity cases
Option result domain is dependency-backed by the admitted Rust option API
PromiseLike result domain is dependency-backed by the admitted Promise.then API
Map.keySet result-domain proof can feed collection membership admission
integer receiver-method result-domain proof can feed chained integer method admission
JS-like Map.keys result is represented as Iterator domain without widening HOF exactness
Java Map.keySet result is represented as Collection domain under exact map proof
Math integer APIs already require integer arguments before admitting the result domain
Map.get value domain depends on container value type and is not a fixed receiver-method result
